title: [Healthcare Toolkit PR-9] Make profile evidence-pack cache readiness report-eligible for Delaware launch systems
description:
Healthcare Toolkit PR-9.5 final MCP remediation review found that hc-mcp doctor --json --check exits ready overall, and health_system_metrics is ready for AHRQ/CMS backbone facts, but workflow profile_evidence_pack remains not report-ready for the Delaware launch systems Bayhealth, Beebe Healthcare, and TidalHealth Peninsula Regional when broader evidence-pack facts are needed. Toolkit PR-8 narrowed persistence to cache-ready source families, so this should be fixed in Healthcare Data MCP rather than worked around in Toolkit.

Affected source families reported by the 2026-06-22 doctor/profile evidence-pack readiness path: cms_cost_report is missing; census_geocoder is missing; state_hospital_reports/de_hospital_discharge is partial; osm_nominatim and official_system_pages_reports via web_intelligence are partial; cms_pecos_public_provider_enrollment is partial; cms_pecos_hospital_chow is partial.

Expected behavior: get_workflow_cache_readiness/build_profile_evidence_pack should either make each public/cacheable source family report-eligible through documented acquisition/promote paths, or explicitly classify unavailable/licensed/manual families with precise non-ready states and next commands so downstream agents can persist unavailable_public/source_conflict/needs_review without treating missing cache data as factual evidence.

Recovery path: implement or repair cache-manager acquisition/promote flows and readiness classification for the listed dataset IDs, add regression coverage for the profile_evidence_pack workflow, then rerun hc-mcp doctor --json --check and profile evidence-pack readiness for Bayhealth, Beebe Healthcare, and TidalHealth Peninsula Regional.
acceptance_criteria: For the three Delaware launch systems, profile_evidence_pack readiness no longer reports public/cacheable source families as missing or partial without a working recovery command; cms_cost_report, census_geocoder/census_acs, de_hospital_discharge/state_hospital_reports, web_intelligence official pages/geocoding, cms_pecos_public_provider_enrollment, and cms_pecos_hospital_chow are each either report-eligible or explicitly classified with a documented non-code/manual/licensed blocker; regression tests cover the readiness classification; the issue links back to Healthcare Toolkit PR-9.5 evidence.
notes: Created from Healthcare Toolkit healthcare-toolkit-ckt.5. Evidence: hc-mcp doctor --json --check on 2026-06-22; Toolkit artifacts 2026-06-22-pr9.2-runtime-verification.json, 2026-06-22-pr9.3-source-ledger-audit.json, and 2026-06-22-pr9.4-readiness-sweep.json show Toolkit final verification passed after narrowing to cache-ready facts, but profile_evidence_pack still has systemic MCP cache-readiness gaps for broader profile population.

title: Fix cache-manager CI lint failures
description: Resolve ruff failures on cache-manager PR: unused imports and loop variable shadowing dataclasses.field.
close_reason: Fixed CI lint failures and merged PR #27 after all GitHub checks passed.

title: Fix cache quarantine current-manifest behavior
description: Quarantine must update the dataset current manifest to corrupt and remove/quarantine published compatibility copies so downstream readers cannot keep consuming quarantined artifacts.
close_reason: Fixed quarantine to update current manifest, remove published compatibility copies, and added regression coverage; full validation passed.

title: Address cache-manager review concerns
description: Fix review findings: verify compatibility copies used by downstream readers, make unchecked workflow cache readiness non-ready, restore README deployment/contribution guidance, and validate with focused/full tests.
close_reason: Fixed review findings: compatibility-copy integrity now gates readiness, unchecked cache_readiness blocks workflows, README safety/contribution guidance restored, and focused/full validation passed.

title: Finish cache-manager reliability review fixes
description: Address remaining cache-manager review findings for end-user reliability: validate real public source headers via registry aliases, enforce manifest checksum/content-length integrity during readiness, and make manual multi-artifact promotion explicit with relative_path in core and MCP APIs.
close_reason: Implemented real-source header aliases, manifest integrity checks, artifact-aware validation, explicit multi-artifact promotion via relative_path, MCP schema update, and regression verification.

title: Fix cache-manager review findings
description: Address six review findings from cache-manager implementation: include untracked implementation in diff, enforce schema/exact identifier validation, require multi-artifact dataset completeness, fix cache-root promotion/rollback confinement, require exact normalized source URLs including redirects, and use severity-based workflow cache status aggregation.
close_reason: Fixed cache-manager review findings: implementation files included in diff, schema/exact identifier validation enforced, multi-artifact readiness hardened, promotion/rollback paths confined, URL allowlist made exact, workflow cache aggregation made severity-based, and regressions verified.

title: Run full verification and reconcile beads
description: Run full pytest, deterministic evals, hc-mcp doctor --json, hc-mcp --list, distribution checks, and Beads export/push. Close implementation beads only after verified.
acceptance_criteria: pytest and eval suites pass; acceptance criteria are demonstrably met; bd-agent-sync push runs after Beads changes.
close_reason: Duplicate distribution children from interrupted Beads creation batch; canonical children are under HDM-cq1.6.

title: Make hospital_competitive_profile report-ready
description: Implement the flagship workflow path for default CCN 390223 and an explicit measure such as clabsi_sir. Preserve exact CCN identity, exact CMS quality measure rows, finance/workforce/claims context where supported, and report fact rows with source metadata and caveats.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow emits evidence, source_metadata, identity, identity_map, and caveats with no placeholders; exact measure rows override adjacent summaries; state-limited or unsupported sources are refused/degraded explicitly.
close_reason: Duplicate workflow children from interrupted Beads creation batch; canonical children are under HDM-cq1.4.

acceptance_criteria: An agent can get an ordered cache plan for hospital_competitive_profile with blockers and next actions; missing data is never treated as a negative factual claim.
close_reason: Duplicate workflow children from interrupted Beads creation batch; canonical children are under HDM-cq1.4.

title: Implement read-only cache-manager tools and resources
description: Implement list_cache_sources, inspect_cache_source, get_workflow_cache_readiness, plan_cache_refresh, get_cache_manifest, and get_cache_lineage plus resources for capabilities, datasets, workflows, policy, and metrics.
acceptance_criteria: Tools return structured responses with blockers and next actions; no raw large data is returned; workflow readiness resolves aliases to canonical dataset IDs.
close_reason: Implemented and verified cache-manager plan work in code, docs, registry/configs, deterministic evals, MCP smoke, doctor JSON, and pytest.

title: Implement local scoped mutating cache-manager tools
description: Implement start_cache_refresh, get_cache_job, validate_cache_source, promote_cache_artifact, quarantine_cache_artifact, and rollback_cache_artifact. Mutations must require registered datasets and local-safe policy.
acceptance_criteria: All required rejection tests pass; jobs expose progress and recovery hints; promotion requires passing validation; quarantine and rollback are single-dataset scoped.
close_reason: Implemented and verified cache-manager plan work in code, docs, registry/configs, deterministic evals, MCP smoke, doctor JSON, and pytest.

title: Add first-class cache-manager MCP server
description: Add a cache-manager server separate from discovery/gateway. It exposes bounded read-only planning/inspection tools and scoped local mutating tools guarded by cache policy. Discovery and remote gateway remain metadata-only/read-only.
acceptance_criteria: cache-manager is registered in CLI/registry/resources/docs/config renderers/smoke tests; read-only tools provide ordered plans; mutating tools are explicit, audited, scoped, bounded, and unavailable from remote metadata gateway.
close_reason: All child implementation tasks completed and verified for health-system cache management.

title: Implement guarded acquisition promotion rollback pipeline
description: Implement local-safe refresh/import pipeline with per-dataset locks, staging directories, content hashes, HTTP redirect revalidation, URL allowlists, byte limits, extension/content-type checks, validation-before-promotion, previous-good rollback, and safe staging cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: Mutating paths reject arbitrary URLs, path traversal, private-network targets, oversized downloads, unknown datasets, unsupported file types, and unsafe redirects; refreshes are idempotent and do not poison current artifacts on failure.
close_reason: Implemented and verified cache-manager plan work in code, docs, registry/configs, deterministic evals, MCP smoke, doctor JSON, and pytest.

title: Implement CacheDatasetSpec catalog adapter
description: Build shared cache-manager specs from the existing server registry, source catalog, workflow source aliases, and doctor cache metadata. Include expected artifacts, TTL, acquisition mode, source period semantics, identity/join keys, required columns, coverage, caveats, and report eligibility rules.
acceptance_criteria: Specs are deterministic, registry-backed, and expose exact identifiers such as CCN, NPI, PECOS, AHRQ system ID, measure ID, UEI/CAGE; no joins are authorized on name/address/web similarity alone.
close_reason: Implemented and verified cache-manager plan work in code, docs, registry/configs, deterministic evals, MCP smoke, doctor JSON, and pytest.

title: Implement manifests validation audit and lineage store
description: Add CacheArtifactManifest, CacheRun, CacheValidationResult, cache audit records, and lineage helpers under a shared cache_manager package. Persist bounded JSON under manifests/datasets, manifests/artifacts, manifests/runs, and audit logs within the configured cache root.
acceptance_criteria: Atomic JSON writes; schema/content/checksum/source-period/coverage validators; report_eligible flag; lineage can explain promoted artifacts and downstream workflow use.
close_reason: Implemented and verified cache-manager plan work in code, docs, registry/configs, deterministic evals, MCP smoke, doctor JSON, and pytest.

title: Unify cache specs manifests validation and guarded acquisition
description: Create the shared cache-management domain model: dataset specs, artifact manifests, run records, validation results, readiness states, audit events, safe acquisition policy, atomic promotion, quarantine, and rollback. Cache readiness means validated source readiness, not file existence.
acceptance_criteria: Specs cover national flagship datasets and source caveats; manifests/runs are atomically written; validators distinguish missing/stale/corrupt/partial/state-limited/manual/licensed/unsupported/env-required; refresh is idempotent and rollback-capable.
close_reason: All child implementation tasks completed and verified for health-system cache management.

acceptance_criteria: bd status shows restored issues; bd ready lists actionable issues; bd export produces .beads/issues.jsonl; bd-agent-sync push succeeds or any remote limitation is recorded.
close_reason: Recovered HDM Beads from intact .beads/dolt/HDM, repaired embedded config and metadata, verified bd list/ready/status/export, and created cache-management task graph.

title: Repair and reconcile HDM Beads tracking
description: Recover repo-local Beads before implementation. Restore prior HDM issue history from the intact Dolt store, repair embedded configuration, verify list/ready/status/export, and create this plan's task graph with dependency edges. Do not create a parallel tracker.
acceptance_criteria: bd list, bd ready, bd status, and bd export succeed from the repo root; prior HDM history is visible or a recovery reason is documented; cache-management epics/tasks exist with dependencies.
close_reason: Beads repair and cache-management graph creation completed.

title: Implement health-system cache management control plane
description: Parent epic for docs/HEALTH_SYSTEM_CACHE_MANAGEMENT_PLAN.md. Build intelligent MCP cache management so an LLM can inspect, plan, acquire, refresh, validate, and safely use public U.S. healthcare caches with provenance, validation status, source periods, identity boundaries, and recovery guidance. Keep discovery and remote gateway read-only; add write-capable cache operations only to a local-safe cache-manager server.
acceptance_criteria: All child epics complete; full pytest and deterministic evals pass; hc-mcp doctor --json reports cache readiness/validation/source periods/report eligibility; hospital_competitive_profile emits report-ready evidence without placeholders.
close_reason: All child implementation tasks completed and verified for health-system cache management.

title: Generalize health-system facility reconciliation beyond Jefferson
description: Make health-system-profiler.reconcile_system_facilities work for non-Jefferson systems by adding a reusable canonical facility ledger path from AHRQ/CMS POS/provider enrollment where available, preserving Jefferson's deterministic merger ledger as a registered special-case resolver. Add tests for generic systems and unsupported/ambiguous cases.
close_reason: Implemented generic AHRQ/CMS facility reconciliation for non-Jefferson systems while preserving Jefferson's curated merger ledger.

title: Development readiness cleanup from registry and data-source acceptance list
close_reason: Implemented development readiness cleanup across MCP and toolkit registries

title: Add shared source catalog resolver and identity normalization
description: Implement the shared foundation required by the 2026-04-23 expansion plans. Add shared/utils/source_catalog.py to resolve CMS datasets from data.cms.gov data.json and CDC Socrata datasets from catalog metadata with fixture-friendly deterministic fallbacks and persisted source manifests. Add shared/utils/identity.py with NPI, CCN, UEI, PAC/enrollment ID, name, address/state/ZIP normalization, and conservative rapidfuzz wrappers. This unblocks CMS PECOS/provider-enrollment and CDC PLACES work without adding more hardcoded dated CMS URLs.
design: Keep source resolution shared and fixture-driven. Do not introduce new one-off CMS release URLs. Source manifests should carry source_url, landing_page, dataset_id, title, modified, fetched_at, etag, last_modified, record_count, and checksum when available.
acceptance_criteria: CMS and Socrata fixture tests pass; identity normalization tests cover invalid/placeholder inputs and conservative fuzzy matching; downstream loaders can call the shared helpers without network in unit tests.
close_reason: Implemented shared source catalog resolver, source manifests, identity normalization, and focused offline tests.

title: Fix cross-server import in physician-referral-network
description: physician_mix.py:173-178 imports from servers.health_system_profiler.data_loaders - fails in standalone mode. Makes analyze_physician_mix and detect_leakage non-functional. Move to shared/ package.

title: Hardcoded CMS URLs will rot when new data releases publish
description: 11/13 servers have CMS download URLs with date-specific paths/UUIDs that break on new releases. Need URL discovery layer querying CMS data catalog API with hardcoded fallback.

title: Wave 10: export live gateway audit evidence
description: Add a compact non-secret audit evidence export for live-gateway policy decisions, including source-claim validation status, blocked/degraded provenance reasons, requested tool scope, and trace IDs.
acceptance_criteria: Live-gateway tests, gateway HTTP integration tests, and source-backed result tests pass; auth, rate-limit, and sensitive-identifier behavior remain unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented live-gateway audit evidence export with trace IDs and full verification.

comments.author: Cole Lyons
comments.text:
Wave 10 implemented compact live-gateway audit evidence export.

Changes:
- added build_audit_evidence_export() in the live gateway policy runner.
- attached audit_evidence to allowed live_gateway_policy metadata with a per-call trace_id.
- attached the same non-secret audit_evidence to gateway audit events, including blocked provenance decisions.
- threaded trace_id through request, scope, rate-limit, result-size, provenance, and response-size policy decisions without changing auth/rate-limit/sensitive-identifier behavior.

Verification:
- .venv/bin/pytest tests/servers/live_gateway/test_policy_runner.py tests/servers/live_gateway/test_server.py -> 57 passed
- .venv/bin/ruff check servers/live_gateway/policy_runner.py servers/live_gateway/server.py tests/servers/live_gateway/test_policy_runner.py tests/servers/live_gateway/test_server.py -> passed
- .venv/bin/python -m py_compile servers/live_gateway/policy_runner.py servers/live_gateway/server.py tests/servers/live_gateway/test_policy_runner.py tests/servers/live_gateway/test_server.py -> passed
- .venv/bin/pytest tests/servers/live_gateway/test_server.py tests/test_gateway_http_integration.py tests/test_source_backed_result.py -> 67 passed
- .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_source_backed_result.py tests/test_mcp_response.py tests/test_workflows.py tests/test_mcp_resources_and_observability.py tests/servers/test_smoke_servers.py -> 83 passed
- .venv/bin/pytest -> 754 passed

title: Wave 7: harden workflow fact manifests
description: Add a shared manifest for workflow fact rows that names value paths, evidence paths, source metadata paths, identity paths, and owning workflow steps, then validate workflow report-ingest templates against it.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow fact manifests cover report-ready templates; workflow/distribution/source-backed gates pass.
close_reason: Implemented workflow fact manifests with Wave 7 gate, cross-wave regression, and full pytest passing.

comments.author: Cole Lyons
comments.text: Wave 7 implemented: added build_workflow_fact_manifest as a static manifest of workflow report fact-row ownership and traceability paths; workflow summaries now expose fact_manifest validation; workflow plans now include report_fact_manifest and a report-ingest-safe manifest copy. Verification: workflow tests passed (33 tests), Wave 7 gate passed (68 tests), cross-wave regression gate passed (83 tests), ruff/py_compile passed on touched Python files, and full pytest passed (751 tests).

title: Wave 6: integrate source capability ledger resources

acceptance_criteria: Standard MCP resources expose a source-ledger resource; capability payloads link to the source-backed contract and ledger; Wave 6 gate passes.
close_reason: Implemented source capability ledger resources and contract links with Wave 6 gate, cross-wave regression, and full pytest passing.

comments.author: Cole Lyons
comments.text: Wave 6 implemented: added standard source-ledger resource registration for every MCP server, exposed source-backed contract/ledger links in capabilities payloads, added server_source_ledger_payload for source capability guidance, and documented the Wave 6-12 post-migration plan. Verification: Wave 6 gate passed (31 tests), cross-wave regression gate passed (82 tests), ruff/py_compile passed on touched Python files, and full pytest passed (750 tests).

title: Improve MCP client compatibility and install docs
description: Audit and update healthcare-data-mcp client packaging and install documentation so Codex CLI/App, Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Desktop Extension/MCPB, and generic MCP clients have clear stdio and HTTP setup paths after the April 2026 expansion. Include new servers, config examples, validation commands, and current remote-gateway boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Docs and examples cover Codex, Claude Code, Claude Desktop, MCPB/Desktop Extension, generic stdio, local Streamable HTTP, and remote gateway usage; examples include provider-enrollment, community-health, and research-trials; focused docs/config tests pass.
close_reason: Updated compatibility docs/examples for Codex CLI/IDE/App, Claude Code, Claude Desktop, MCPB/Desktop Extension, generic MCP clients, local HTTP, and remote gateway usage; added validation tests.

title: Run full verification gates for April 2026 expansion
description: Run the expected local gate after implementation: ruff check ., pytest -q, python3 -m compileall -q servers shared tests smoke_test.py, focused public-records/provider/community/research tests, and optional live source smoke tests for CMS, CDC, NIH, ClinicalTrials.gov, OIG LEIE metadata, and SAM only when SAM_GOV_API_KEY is set.
acceptance_criteria: All feasible gates pass. Any skipped live checks, missing credentials, upstream failures, or non-release-blocking gaps are documented with follow-up beads.
close_reason: Verified with pytest, ruff, compileall, docker compose config, .mcp.json validation, and launcher listing after review fixes.

title: Review and fix provider/community/research/compliance expansion
description: Perform a second-pass review after implementation agents finish. Check architecture consistency, structured response conventions, source metadata/caveat language, batch limits, API-key handling, test coverage, docs, and obvious regressions. Fix issues directly where scoped and create follow-up beads for deferred work.
acceptance_criteria: Review findings are resolved or tracked; ruff, pytest, compileall, and targeted live smoke checks pass or documented blockers are filed.
close_reason: Review agents found and fixed compliance, new-server, and integration issues; full local verification passed after review.

title: Implement LEIE cache parser and query layer
description: Build the public-records LEIE data layer from docs/plans/2026-04-23-hhs-oig-leie-exclusions-tooling.md. Download/cache UPDATED.csv, parse all columns as strings, normalize placeholder NPI/date values, write zstd Parquet plus meta JSON, refresh by ETag/Last-Modified/monthly TTL, and expose query helpers for NPI, individual, entity, batch screening, and metadata. Do not use verify=False and do not store SSNs/EINs.
design: Keep LEIE inside servers/public_records/data_loaders.py. Canonical URL is https://oig.hhs.gov/exclusions/downloadables/UPDATED.csv. If HEAD fails and cache exists under stale threshold, serve stale metadata rather than failing screening workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture parser/query tests pass for exact NPI, individual filters, entity scoring, metadata, missing layout columns, and placeholder normalization.
close_reason: Implemented LEIE cache/parser/query layer, structured models/tools, and fixture-based tests. Verified with focused pytest and ruff in .venv.

title: Expose LEIE public-records tools and structured models
description: Add LEIE Pydantic models and public-records FastMCP tools: check_leie_npi, search_leie_individual, search_leie_entity, screen_leie_batch, and get_leie_metadata. Responses must include source metadata, match basis, match score where relevant, OIG verification caveat language, and avoid cleared/verified-not-excluded wording.
design: Use structured_output=True and existing shared.utils.mcp_response conventions. Cap batch size at 100. Validate 10-digit NPI and reject 0000000000. Name matches are potential_match; exact NPI is strong_potential_match.
acceptance_criteria: Server tests pass for validation, exact-match responses, batch caps, source metadata, and no-download metadata behavior via monkeypatched loaders.
close_reason: Implemented LEIE cache/parser/query layer, structured models/tools, and fixture-based tests. Verified with focused pytest and ruff in .venv.

title: Return structured MCP tool results instead of JSON strings
description: Most tools return json.dumps(...) strings and encode errors as successful text payloads. Migrate high-value tools to typed dict/Pydantic returns where FastMCP can emit structuredContent/output schemas, and raise tool errors for invalid inputs/runtime failures.
close_reason: Migrated tool servers to FastMCP structured output, added shared response helpers, updated stale tests, and verified structured schemas across launcher modules.

title: Add remote MCP gateway with auth for OpenAI and Claude connectors
description: Current servers are local stdio/local HTTP only. Add a production remote MCP gateway with HTTPS, OAuth/auth, origin validation, scoped access, and OpenAI-compatible search/fetch where datasets are used as retrievable knowledge.
close_reason: Implemented local-safe remote MCP gateway skeleton with auth/origin helpers, OpenAI-compatible search/fetch metadata tools, security docs, and focused auth tests.

title: Hardening pass for MCP packaging, bugs, and agent best practices
description: Audit healthcare-data-mcp for bugs, repo organization, MCP packaging/client best practices, and model-friendly tool design. Implement scoped fixes and document remaining follow-up work.
close_reason: Implemented hardening pass: package build, launcher/docs, missing shared helpers/deps, local bind defaults, cleanup, and validation.

title: Prepare repository metadata for public release
description: Public release readiness work covering repository metadata and baseline community docs that are currently missing from the checked-out branch. Add release-facing files such as README, LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING, SECURITY, and package metadata needed for GitHub and PyPI-style presentation.

title: Universal MCP client installation configs
description: Create ready-to-use configs for Claude Code (.mcp.json), OpenCode, Codex CLI, OpenClaw. One-command install scripts, Docker and pip options. Trivially easy setup.

title: Create comprehensive README.md
description: No README at project root. Need: overview, architecture diagram, per-server tool list, install instructions (pip/Docker/MCP configs), API key setup guide, dev workflow.

title: Add unit tests for 12 untested servers
description: Only health-system-profiler has pytest coverage. Other 12 servers have zero unit tests. Need mocked pytest suites for all, priority on servers with external API deps.

title: Thread safety: add asyncio.Lock to global DataFrame caches
description: data_loaders.py:36-38 uses global DataFrames as cache with no locking. Concurrent MCP requests race on global writes under SSE transport.

title: Fix USAspending fiscal year calculation bug
description: usaspending_client.py:28 uses datetime.now().year as FY but federal FY runs Oct-Sep. In April 2026 it searches FY2027 instead of FY2026.

title: Fix SEC EDGAR User-Agent placeholder email
description: edgar_client.py:14 defaults to support@example.com. SEC blocks fake addresses. Remove default, raise error at startup if not set.

title: Fix f-string SQL injection in DuckDB view creation
description: 6 servers construct DuckDB SQL with f-strings for Parquet paths. Use parameterized path syntax instead. Affects: price-transparency, workforce-analytics, claims-analytics, public-records, web-intelligence, geo-demographics.

title: Add HTTP retry/backoff to all 13 servers
description: No server implements retry logic. CMS returns 503s during maintenance. Add tenacity or manual exponential backoff with jitter.

title: Add cache TTL to 9 servers missing it
description: Only geo-demographics has 90-day cache TTL. Other 9 servers cache data forever. CMS updates quarterly. Port geo-demographics pattern to all.

title: Wave 12: complete operational rollout and regression closure
description: Update operator-facing docs, generated artifacts, doctor output, and release notes so the post-migration contract is discoverable, then run the full regression suite.
acceptance_criteria: Operator docs and generated artifacts reference the post-migration contract; full pytest passes; post-migration epic closes only after Waves 6-12 are complete.

title: Wave 11: add source-backed agent eval scenarios
description: Add deterministic eval scenarios for source-substitution failures, missing cache/source-status recovery, workflow handoffs, and live-gateway provenance refusals.
acceptance_criteria: Eval scenarios produce structured remediation hints; cache eval, workflow, and MCP response gates pass.
close_reason: Added source-backed agent eval scenarios with structured remediation hints and full verification.

comments.author: Cole Lyons
comments.text:
Wave 11 added deterministic source-backed agent eval scenarios.

Changes:
- added source_substitution_refusal, missing_source_status_recovery, workflow_handoff_traceability, and live_gateway_provenance_refusal scenarios.
- added remediation_hint to every eval row so failures are actionable for coding agents.
- locked scenario inventory and key structured details in tests.

Verification:
- .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_cache_manager_evals.py -> 1 passed
- .venv/bin/ruff check scripts/evals/cache_manager_eval.py tests/test_cache_manager_evals.py -> passed
- .venv/bin/python -m py_compile scripts/evals/cache_manager_eval.py tests/test_cache_manager_evals.py -> passed
- .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_cache_manager_evals.py tests/test_workflows.py tests/test_mcp_response.py -> 58 passed
- .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_source_backed_result.py tests/test_mcp_response.py tests/test_workflows.py tests/test_mcp_resources_and_observability.py tests/servers/test_smoke_servers.py -> 83 passed
- .venv/bin/pytest -> 754 passed

title: Wave 9: route identity conflicts for review
description: Make cross-server identity conflicts explicit when exact identifiers disagree or candidate context is insufficient, and add review-routing fields for downstream agents.
acceptance_criteria: Facility/system/enrollment/financial/public-record/web workflow joins expose conflict and review-routing semantics; Wave 9 gate passes.
close_reason: Implemented workflow identity review routing with focused, wave, cross-wave, and full-suite verification.

comments.author: Cole Lyons
comments.text:
Wave 9 implemented identity review routing on workflow identity maps.

Changes:
- identity_map now derives review_routing from the existing resolution_plan and merge_policy.
- exact identifier conflicts, candidate context review, and missing exact identifiers are surfaced as explicit routes.
- per-step review routes preserve qualified_tool, fields, conflict checks, and identity output paths for cross-server joins.

Verification:
- .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_workflows.py -> 33 passed
- .venv/bin/ruff check shared/utils/workflows.py tests/test_workflows.py -> passed
- .venv/bin/python -m py_compile shared/utils/workflows.py tests/test_workflows.py -> passed
- .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_workflows.py tests/servers/health_system_profiler/test_generic_reconciliation.py tests/servers/provider_enrollment/test_server.py tests/servers/public_records/test_regulatory_records.py -> 56 passed
- .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_source_backed_result.py tests/test_mcp_response.py tests/test_workflows.py tests/test_mcp_resources_and_observability.py tests/servers/test_smoke_servers.py -> 83 passed
- .venv/bin/pytest -> 753 passed

title: Wave 8: normalize server source-status payloads
description: Normalize cache/source status payloads across live and metadata servers while preserving existing response shapes.
acceptance_criteria: Source period, cache freshness, source URL, retrieval method, and caveat fields are present or explicitly unavailable; doctor/discovery/smoke gates pass.
close_reason: Implemented source-status normalization helper and doctor cache readiness integration with Wave 8 gate, cross-wave regression, and full pytest passing.

comments.author: Cole Lyons
comments.text: Wave 8 implemented first normalization slice: added shared.utils.source_status.normalize_source_status to make source_url, source_period, cache_status, cache_freshness, retrieval_method, and caveat explicit; doctor cache readiness examples now include normalized source_status without changing discovery/server payload shapes. Verification: source-status/doctor focused tests passed (17 tests), Wave 8 gate passed (46 tests), cross-wave regression gate passed (83 tests), ruff/py_compile passed on touched Python files, and full pytest passed (753 tests).

title: Wave 5: deepen modules behind stable source-backed result contract
description: After traceability gates are stable, deepen the public source catalog, health-system profiling, public-records implementation, live-gateway policy runner, and tabular normalization modules behind the source-backed result contract.
acceptance_criteria: Deepened modules preserve source-backed result shapes; full pytest suite passes; no new report-ready result shape bypasses source claim path validation.
close_reason: Wave 5 named deepening targets completed with public-records source-claim split, prior catalog/tabular/live-gateway slices, and full pytest suite passing.

comments.author: Cole Lyons
comments.text: Wave 5 live-gateway policy runner slice implemented: extracted LiveToolSpec, caveat classification, policy attachment, provenance evaluation, and audit provenance fields into servers/live_gateway/policy_runner.py; doctor static validation now follows the extracted runner. Verification: focused live-gateway/source-backed gate passed (69 tests), Wave 5 cross-server regression suite passed (81 tests), ruff/py_compile passed on touched files, full pytest suite passed (744 tests). Leaving HDM-c3w.8 open for remaining Wave 5 targets: public source catalog, health-system profiling, public-records implementation split, and tabular source normalization.

comments.author: Cole Lyons
comments.text: Wave 5 catalog/tabular slice implemented: added shared.utils.tabular_normalization for stable source column/key normalization and string-safe CSV reads; migrated public-records data loaders/server and health-system profiler CSV reads to the shared tabular seam; added PublicSourceCatalog as an object-style resolver around existing CMS/Socrata catalog functions. Verification: target gate passed (92 tests), Wave 5 cross-server regression suite passed (81 tests), ruff/py_compile passed on touched files, full pytest suite passed (747 tests). HDM-c3w.8 remains open for the larger public-records implementation split/deepening audit.

comments.author: Cole Lyons
comments.text: Wave 5 public-records split slice implemented: extracted public-records source-claim construction into servers/public_records/source_claims.py and added direct contract tests for default evidence/source metadata paths, identity paths, dataset IDs, and row evidence paths. Existing public-records server call sites now route through the extracted helper without changing response shapes. Verification: public-records focused gate passed (43 tests), Wave 5 cross-server regression suite passed (81 tests), ruff/py_compile passed on touched files, full pytest suite passed (749 tests). This completes the named Wave 5 targets while leaving the parent epic open for any future waves that get specified.

title: Wave 3D: migrate health-system, web, research, and referral servers
description: Migrate health-system-profiler, web-intelligence, research-trials, and physician-referral-network outputs to boundary-ready source claim paths across system reconciliation, profile evidence pack, research, and referral workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Group D server tests plus tests/test_workflows.py pass; system/profile/research/referral workflow outputs validate source claim paths.
close_reason: Wave 3D migrated health-system, web, research, and referral outputs to boundary-ready source claim paths. Gate passed: .venv/bin/pytest tests/servers/health_system_profiler/test_server.py tests/servers/health_system_profiler/test_profile_evidence_pack.py tests/servers/health_system_profiler/test_system_metrics.py tests/servers/web_intelligence/test_search_client.py tests/servers/research_trials/test_server.py tests/servers/physician_referral_network/test_server.py tests/test_workflows.py

title: Wave 3C: migrate financial, claims, and workforce servers
description: Migrate financial-intelligence, claims-analytics, and workforce-analytics outputs to boundary-ready source claim paths across finance, competitive, and referral workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Group C server tests plus tests/test_workflows.py pass; finance and competitive workflow outputs validate source claim paths.
close_reason: Wave 3C migrated financial, claims, and workforce outputs to boundary-ready source claim paths. Gate passed: .venv/bin/pytest tests/servers/financial_intelligence/test_server.py tests/servers/claims_analytics/test_server.py tests/servers/workforce_analytics/test_workforce_data.py tests/test_workflows.py

title: Wave 3B: migrate ownership, compliance, and public-record servers
description: Migrate provider-enrollment and public-records outputs to boundary-ready source claim paths, preserving compliance and ownership workflow semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Group B server tests plus tests/test_workflows.py pass; compliance and ownership workflow outputs validate source claim paths.
close_reason: Wave 3B migrated provider-enrollment and public-records outputs to boundary-ready source claim paths. Gate passed: .venv/bin/pytest tests/servers/provider_enrollment/test_server.py tests/servers/public_records/test_leie_server.py tests/servers/public_records/test_sam_exclusions_server.py tests/servers/public_records/test_regulatory_records.py tests/servers/public_records/test_cyber_enrichment.py tests/servers/public_records/test_federal_search_records.py tests/test_workflows.py

title: Wave 3A: migrate quality and facility fact servers
description: Migrate hospital-quality, service-area, geo-demographics, and drive-time outputs to boundary-ready source claim paths without changing shared contract modules.
acceptance_criteria: Group A server tests plus tests/test_workflows.py pass; migrated report-ready outputs validate source claim paths.
close_reason: Wave 3A migrated quality/facility fact servers to boundary-ready source claim paths. Gate passed: .venv/bin/pytest tests/servers/hospital_quality/test_server.py tests/servers/service_area/test_server.py tests/servers/geo_demographics/test_server.py tests/servers/drive_time/test_drive_time_server.py tests/test_workflows.py

title: Add setup cache doctor for manual MCP datasets
description: Add hc-mcp-setup support for checking and importing manual 340B, HIPAA breach, and DocGraph cache files so users are not left with opaque missing-cache tool failures.
acceptance_criteria: hc-mcp-setup reports manual cache status, imports 340B JSON and HIPAA breach CSV, converts/imports DocGraph shared-patient data, documents the flow, and has focused tests.
close_reason: Implemented hc-mcp-setup --cache-status plus manual imports for 340B JSON, HIPAA breach CSV, DocGraph CSV/Parquet; documented in README; verified with ruff and tests/test_env_setup.py.

title: Polish README and release notes for MCP setup release
description: Improve README presentation and clarity after the April 2026 expansion and MCP client setup improvements. Update release notes to mention the setup wizard, env-file loading, client compatibility examples, MCPB GUI fields, and validation coverage.
acceptance_criteria: README is clearer and more polished without overclaiming; release notes mention the MCP setup/client compatibility changes; markdown links are valid; docs-focused checks pass.
close_reason: README polish and release-note setup documentation completed; verification passed

title: Write release notes for April 2026 data expansion
description: After verification, write concise release notes summarizing LEIE, SAM Exclusions, provider enrollment/ownership, community health, research/trials, discovery/docs, test coverage, and any known limitations or follow-up beads.
acceptance_criteria: Release notes are committed in the repo using the existing docs/changelog pattern or a suitable docs release-notes file, and accurately reflect verified behavior.
close_reason: Added docs/release-notes/2026-04-23-provider-community-research-compliance-expansion.md with verified scope, gates, and known limits.

title: Register new servers, client config, Docker, and smoke tests
description: Wire provider-enrollment, community-health, and research-trials into servers/_launcher.py, .mcp.json, docker-compose.yml, README, MCP client docs, discovery/gateway metadata where applicable, and smoke_test.py. Keep public-records on port 8013, add new ports 8017/8018/8019, reuse healthcare-cache volume, and keep gateway remote-safe.
acceptance_criteria: Import/launcher tests pass, smoke tests include optional live checks guarded by env vars/API keys, docs list the new tools and ports, and no occupied Plumbob ports are introduced.
close_reason: Registered provider-enrollment, community-health, and research-trials across launcher, Docker, MCP config, docs, smoke hooks, and focused tests.

title: Implement SAM.gov Exclusions public-records tooling
description: After LEIE data/tools are in place, add SAM.gov Exclusions API v4 tooling to public-records: search_sam_exclusions, check_sam_exclusion_identifier, screen_sam_exclusions_batch, get_sam_exclusions_metadata, models, API-key handling, source metadata, and tests. Use JSON API for search/detail; leave tokenized CSV export for future bulk mirror mode.
acceptance_criteria: SAM exclusions tests pass with mocked API responses, missing API key behavior is structured and non-crashing, batch size is capped, and discovery/docs include sam_gov_exclusions.
close_reason: Implemented scoped SAM.gov Exclusions JSON API client, public-records tools, models, and mocked tests. Discovery/docs/client registration were explicitly out of scope for this slice and remain covered by downstream registration work.

title: Implement research activity profile and organization matching
description: Add conservative organization matching and profile_research_activity to combine NIH funding and ClinicalTrials.gov activity for an organization/facility/state. Avoid silently merging hospitals, universities, foundations, and health systems with similar names; label candidate matches clearly unless UEI/IPF/exact sponsor identity is present.
acceptance_criteria: Research profile tests pass for conservative matching, aggregation by year/PI/institute/activity/status, and source-specific caveats.
close_reason: Implemented conservative organization matching and combined research activity profile without silent ambiguous organization merges. Verified with profile/matching fixture tests plus focused research-trials test suite.

title: Implement ClinicalTrials.gov client and tools
description: Create research-trials ClinicalTrials.gov v2 client and FastMCP tools for search_clinical_trials and get_clinical_trial. Support query, sponsor, condition, intervention, location, status, phase, fields, page_size, and page_token. Preserve nextPageToken, API version/dataTimestamp metadata, sponsor/collaborators, status, contacts/locations, dates, enrollment, phases, and links.
acceptance_criteria: ClinicalTrials.gov client/tool tests pass for query construction, field selection, pagination token handling, version metadata, and fixture study detail normalization.
close_reason: Implemented ClinicalTrials.gov v2 client/tools with version/dataTimestamp metadata, page token handling, normalization, and offline fixture tests. Verified with pytest tests/servers/research_trials -q, ruff check, and python3 compileall.

title: Implement NIH RePORTER client and tools
description: Create research-trials NIH RePORTER client and FastMCP tools for search_nih_projects, get_nih_project, and profile_research_funding. Support project search criteria for org, UEI, PI, text, fiscal years, activity codes, agencies, limit, and offset. Preserve project numbers, APPL IDs, awards, PIs, org identifiers, institutes, terms, publications where available, and source metadata.
acceptance_criteria: RePORTER client/tool tests pass for request construction, pagination/offset, fixture response normalization, project detail, and funding profile aggregation.
close_reason: Implemented NIH RePORTER client/tools, funding profile aggregation, structured models, and offline fixture tests. Verified with pytest tests/servers/research_trials -q, ruff check, and python3 compileall.

title: Implement CDC PLACES client, loaders, and measure metadata
description: Create community-health loader/client layer for CDC PLACES 2025 county/place/tract/ZCTA datasets and data dictionary. Discover Socrata dataset IDs by title/release instead of treating current IDs as permanent. Normalize value types, confidence intervals, population, geolocation fields, and cache bulk data to Parquet where useful.
acceptance_criteria: Community health client/loader tests pass for catalog resolution, measure metadata, filtered Socrata query building, and fixture profile rows.
close_reason: Implemented CDC PLACES catalog/Socrata client, row normalization, optional Parquet cache, and fixture-backed loader tests.

title: Implement provider-enrollment data loaders and cache manifests
description: Create provider-enrollment loader layer for CMS Medicare FFS Public Provider Enrollment, Hospital/SNF enrollments, all owners, and CHOW files. Use the shared CMS source resolver, persist source manifests, normalize to Parquet, preserve original CMS column names and snake-case aliases, and support fixture-driven tests without live CMS calls.
acceptance_criteria: Provider enrollment loader tests pass for CMS source resolution, fixture CSV/API parsing, Parquet cache writes, manifest metadata, and join-key normalization.
close_reason: Implemented provider-enrollment CMS loaders, normalization, Parquet manifests, and fixture-driven tests.

title: Implement community-health MCP tools and service-area profiles
description: Create community-health FastMCP server on port 8018 with list_places_measures, search_places, get_places_profile, compare_places, and get_market_community_profile. Responses must label PLACES values as community estimates, expose confidence intervals, and avoid patient-level interpretation.
acceptance_criteria: Community-health server/profile tests pass; launcher registration imports; compare/profile tools return structured source metadata and missing-data notes.
close_reason: Implemented community-health FastMCP tools and service-area profile aggregation with structured source metadata and missing-data notes.

title: Implement provider-enrollment MCP tools and ownership graph
description: Create provider-enrollment FastMCP server on port 8017 with search_provider_enrollment, get_provider_enrollment_detail, get_facility_ownership, trace_owner_network, search_change_of_ownership, and profile_provider_control. Implement bounded ownership graph output with depth cap 3 and conservative identity joins.
acceptance_criteria: Provider server and ownership graph tests pass; launcher registration imports; tools return structured responses with source metadata and bounded graph output.
close_reason: Implemented provider-enrollment MCP tools and bounded NetworkX ownership graph helpers with focused tests.

title: Register LEIE discovery metadata and docs
description: Add hhs_oig_leie to discovery metadata, expected cache files, README/public-records docs, and discovery docs where applicable. Ensure dataset search/cache-status surfaces LEIE source URLs, schema identity fields, common fields, join keys, and workflows for provider/vendor/enrollment screening.
acceptance_criteria: Discovery metadata tests pass and docs mention LEIE tools, source caveats, cache files, and verification limitations.
close_reason: Registered LEIE discovery metadata, cache expectations, and docs.

title: Add SAM.gov Exclusions tooling to public-records
description: After LEIE lands, add SAM.gov Exclusions API v4 search and screening tools to public-records with source metadata, API-key handling, batch limits, discovery metadata, and tests.
close_reason: Implemented SAM.gov Exclusions API v4 tooling, public-records tools, mocked tests, discovery metadata, docs, and registration.

title: Implement CDC PLACES community health server
description: Build the community-health MCP server from the 2026-04-23 expansion plan: Socrata source discovery, PLACES measure metadata, county/place/tract/ZCTA search, profile/compare tools, service-area profile workflow, and tests.
close_reason: Implemented community-health server with CDC PLACES catalog discovery, measure/search/profile/compare tools, registration, docs, and tests. HDM-d0i is unrelated to CDC PLACES and remains open for existing CMS URL migration.

title: Implement CMS PECOS provider enrollment and ownership server
description: Build the provider-enrollment MCP server from the 2026-04-23 expansion plan: CMS public provider enrollment, hospital/SNF enrollment and owner files, CHOW data, ownership graph queries, launcher/Docker/client registration, and tests.
close_reason: Implemented provider-enrollment server with CMS PECOS-derived enrollment, hospital/SNF ownership and CHOW loaders, bounded ownership graph tools, registration, docs, and tests. HDM-d0i remains open for migrating pre-existing hardcoded CMS URLs elsewhere.

title: Implement NIH RePORTER and ClinicalTrials.gov research server
description: Build the research-trials MCP server from the 2026-04-23 expansion plan: NIH RePORTER project/profile tools, ClinicalTrials.gov v2 search/detail tools, organization matching, source metadata, and tests.
close_reason: Implemented research-trials server with NIH RePORTER, ClinicalTrials.gov, conservative org matching, tests, docs, and registration.

title: Implement HHS OIG LEIE exclusions tooling
description: Implement the LEIE tooling described in docs/plans/2026-04-23-hhs-oig-leie-exclusions-tooling.md: cache UPDATED.csv, add structured public-records tools, discovery metadata, and focused tests.
design: Use the existing public-records server rather than a new server. Canonical source is https://oig.hhs.gov/exclusions/downloadables/UPDATED.csv cached as public-records/leie_current.parquet with metadata. Do not automate SSN/EIN verification or use verify=False for TLS.
acceptance_criteria: LEIE cache metadata and Parquet loader are implemented; public-records exposes NPI, individual, entity, batch, and metadata tools; discovery includes hhs_oig_leie; focused pytest suite passes.
close_reason: Implemented LEIE data/cache/tools, discovery/docs, tests, and verification caveats inside public-records.

title: Plan PECOS, PLACES, NIH/ClinicalTrials, and exclusions tooling
description: Investigate target public data sources and current MCP server architecture, then produce an implementation plan for adding provider enrollment/ownership, community health, research/trials, and exclusion screening tooling.
design: Plan written at docs/plans/2026-04-23-provider-community-research-compliance-expansion.md. Follow-up implementation beads filed: HDM-aev, HDM-4qa, HDM-t1u, HDM-0xl; existing LEIE implementation bead HDM-1oz and CMS source freshness bead HDM-d0i remain prerequisites where applicable.
close_reason: Plan written at docs/plans/2026-04-23-provider-community-research-compliance-expansion.md; follow-up implementation beads filed and linked.

title: Plan HHS OIG LEIE exclusions tooling
description: Investigate HHS OIG LEIE data sources and local MCP server patterns, then write a durable implementation plan for adding exclusions tooling.
close_reason: Plan written at docs/plans/2026-04-23-hhs-oig-leie-exclusions-tooling.md and implementation follow-up filed as HDM-1oz.

title: Package Claude Desktop MCPB and Codex config examples
description: Add distributable Claude Desktop .mcpb metadata/build flow and checked-in sample Codex MCP config snippets so clients do not have to infer install modes from README text.
close_reason: Added Claude Desktop stdio examples, Codex config examples, and MCPB packaging skeleton/build script.

title: Add MCP resources and prompts for dataset discovery
description: Repo is tools-only. Add resources for dataset catalog, schemas, source URLs, cache status, and setup runbooks, plus prompt templates for common healthcare market-analysis workflows.
close_reason: Added metadata-only discovery server with dataset resources, cache status, prompts, launcher/Docker/client wiring, docs, and tests.

title: Verify ProPublica field mapping in financial-intelligence
description: server.py:72 assigns total_expenses from revenue_amount and total_revenue from income_amount. May be swapped. Verify against ProPublica API docs.

title: Geo-demographics batch fetches all 33K ZCTAs unnecessarily
description: census_client.py:237-244 for batches >10 ZCTAs fetches ALL ~33,000 ZCTAs (~50MB). Should chunk into groups of 10 with targeted requests.

title: Create .env setup script with validation
description: No .env file exists. Add setup script: copies .env.example, validates keys, reports degraded servers, optionally prompts for entry.

title: Add CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions
description: No CI/CD exists. Need GitHub Actions: ruff lint, type check, unit tests with mock APIs, smoke tests, Docker build verification.
notes: CI exists (.github/workflows/ci.yml) but needs type checking + broader unit test coverage

title: Extend smoke tests to cover all 13 servers
description: smoke_test.py only covers original 7 servers. 6 newer servers have zero coverage: health-system-profiler, physician-referral-network, workforce-analytics, claims-analytics, public-records, web-intelligence.

title: Google CSE quota management for web-intelligence
description: 100 queries/day free. Single analysis burns 10-15. Need batching, alternative search backends, exponential backoff, per-session quota counter.

title: Add MRF disk space guard for price-transparency
description: MRF files 1-10 GB. No Content-Length check, no max size, no progress. Add configurable max (10 GB default).

title: Wire up HSA crosswalk for leakage detection
description: detect_leakage hard-codes out_of_network_in_area_pct to 0.0. HSA crosswalk infrastructure exists but never called. Wire it up.

title: DocGraph data requires manual loading for physician-referral-network
description: map_referral_network and detect_leakage gated behind manual CSV load. 2 of 5 tools non-functional for new installs. Expose as MCP tool or automate.

title: Missing ACGME static data file for workforce-analytics
description: data/ contains only .gitkeep. get_residency_programs is non-functional. Bundle data, provide download script, or scrape ACGME at startup.

title: Extract duplicate utility code into shared module
description: Functions _is_cache_valid, _get_con, _detect_columns, _find_col, _s, get_cache_path reimplemented identically across servers. Extract to shared/utils/. Also consolidate duplicate os imports.

title: Connection pooling for httpx across all servers
description: Every API call creates new httpx.AsyncClient. Web-intelligence makes 2-4 per tool. Add shared session with connection pooling.

title: Expand GPO directory in web-intelligence
description: gpo_directory.csv only 578 bytes (~5-6 GPOs). Healthcare has dozens (Vizient, Premier, HealthTrust, Intalere, HPG, etc.). Expand to all major GPOs.

title: Duplicate get_hospital_info and get_facility in cms-facility
description: Both do identical CCN lookup returning Facility model. Remove one or differentiate.

title: Unused complications data loader in hospital-quality
description: data_loaders.py:80-83 load_complications() defined but no tool uses it. Wire up or remove.

title: Hospital General Info downloaded independently by 3 servers
description: service-area, drive-time, cms-facility each download/cache hospital_general_info.csv independently. Consolidate to shared cache.

title: Deduplicate financial profile logic between hospital-quality and cms-facility
description: Both implement get_facility_financials from same Cost Report with nearly identical code. Extract to shared/.

title: include_projections parameter is no-op in workforce-analytics
description: server.py:45 accepts include_projections: bool but it is never used. Either implement or remove.
